title: Count the Number of Complete Components
titleCn: 统计完全连通分量的数量

testcases.input: n = 6, edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,2],[3,4]] Output: 3 Explanation: From the picture above, one can see that all of the components of this graph are complete.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: From the picture above, one can see that all of the components of this graph are complete.

testcases.input: n = 6, edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,2],[3,4],[3,5]] Output: 1 Explanation: The component containing vertices 0, 1, and 2 is complete since there is an edge between every pair of two vertices. On the other hand, the component containing vertices 3, 4, and 5 is not complete since there is no edge between vertices 4 and 5. Thus, the number of complete components in this graph is 1.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: The component containing vertices 0, 1, and 2 is complete since there is an edge between every pair of two vertices. On the other hand, the component containing vertices 3, 4, and 5 is not complete since there is no edge between vertices 4 and 5. Thus, the number of complete components in this graph is 1.
